description.en: A big, softly spiraled sweet roll swirled with cinnamon and brown sugar, usually finished with a thick cream cheese icing. Descended from Scandinavian immigrant baking, it became an American bakery icon for weekend mornings and Christmas Day.
description.de: Eine große, weich aufgerollte süße Schnecke mit Zimt-Zucker-Füllung, meist dick mit Frischkäse-Guss bestrichen. Von skandinavischen Einwanderern abstammend, wurde sie zur amerikanischen Bäckerei-Ikone für Wochenendmorgen und den Weihnachtstag.
description.es: Un gran rollo dulce enrollado con canela y azúcar moreno, casi siempre cubierto de un espeso glaseado de queso crema. Con raíces en la repostería escandinava, se volvió un ícono de panadería estadounidense para las mañanas de fin de semana y Navidad.
description.fr: Un grand rouleau moelleux à la cannelle et au sucre roux, le plus souvent nappé d'un épais glaçage au fromage frais. Hérité de la pâtisserie scandinave, il est devenu une icône des boulangeries américaines pour les matins de week-end et Noël.

origin.note: Descends from Scandinavian immigrant sweet-bun baking (kin to Swedish kanelbullar) but grew larger, softer and more heavily iced into its own distinct American bakery-case identity.

ingredients.note: bread or all-purpose flour, for the dough
or heavy cream, for the icing

keeping.note: Best day one; the cream cheese icing means leftovers should be refrigerated rather than left out.

method.title: Bloom the yeast
method.summary: Warm the milk, stir in a spoonful of the sugar and the yeast, and let it sit until foamy, about 5-10 minutes.

method.title: Mix and knead
method.summary: Beat in the rest of the sugar, the butter, eggs and salt, then work in the flour and knead until you have a smooth, elastic dough.

method.title: First rise
method.summary: Cover and let the dough double in size at room temperature, roughly 1.5-2 hours.

method.title: Fill and roll
method.summary: Roll the dough into a large rectangle, spread with soft butter, scatter the cinnamon-brown-sugar filling to the edges, then roll into a tight log and slice into 12 rounds.

method.title: Shape, then chill or proof
method.summary: Arrange the rolls in a baking dish. Either let them puff for about an hour at room temperature, or cover and refrigerate overnight for a slow rise, bringing them back to room temperature before baking.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Bake at around 177°C (350°F) for 25-28 minutes until golden, tenting loosely with foil if they colour too quickly.

method.title: Ice and serve
method.summary: Beat the cream cheese, butter, powdered sugar, milk and vanilla into a smooth icing and spread it over the rolls while still warm.

source.adaptation: formula.* covers the dough only; the cinnamon-sugar filling and cream cheese icing are itemized but excluded from the percentages. Salt nudged up slightly from source for balance.
